summary:
This patient presents with signs consistent with decompensated cirrhosis secondary to long-standing hepatitis C infection. Key findings include hepatic encephalopathy causing cognitive impairment and spontaneous bacterial peritonitis leading to systemic illness.

The summary above correctly identifies the core pathophysiological mechanisms linking hepatology complications—specifically hepatic encephalopathy and SBT—to acute presentation in advanced liver failure.
